_meta.description: Populate origin, source_category, inference_note, and anchor_verses for all 211 active registry words; correct source_list values from the combined inner-being words document.
_meta.reason: The combined inner-being words document (inner_being_words_combined.md) records the analytical reasoning for each word's inclusion across three source lists and eight categories. 26 words added during programme development are classified as Programme Addition. Four new columns are required in word_registry before this patch can be applied.
import_order: 1. Execute schema_changes block first — adds four new TEXT columns to word_registry
2. Execute word_registry_update — updates all 211 rows with metadata values
_meta.new_columns_note: Four new columns added to word_registry: origin, source_category, inference_note, anchor_verses. See schema_changes block for DDL.

schema_changes._instruction: Apply BEFORE word_registry_update. For each entry in columns_to_add, execute: ALTER TABLE word_registry ADD COLUMN <name> <type>. If the column already exists, skip silently (use try/except or check PRAGMA table_info first).

columns_to_add.name: origin
columns_to_add.description: Provenance of the word in the registry. Values: 'original_list' (from the 181-word research document) | 'programme_addition' (added during Session A development).

columns_to_add.name: source_category
columns_to_add.description: Functional category from the original research document. Values: Moral/Conscience | Affective/Emotional | Cognitive/Mind | Relational/Social | Identity/Selfhood | Character/Disposition | Volitional/Will | Spiritual/God-ward | Anthropological/Structural | Volitional/Capacity.

columns_to_add.name: inference_note
columns_to_add.description: For Low Confidence / Inferred words only: the analytical reasoning for inclusion — why the concept is present in the biblical data even when the English word does not have a direct biblical equivalent. Null for all other words.

columns_to_add.name: anchor_verses
columns_to_add.description: The 1-3 key Scripture references identified as most theologically significant for this word. Semi-colon separated. Drawn from STEP Bible Suggestion notes in the research document.

rows.inference_note: Ambition is a morally neutral modern term that Scripture handles through two distinct concepts — selfish ambition (always negative) and godly zeal/aspiration (positive). The neutral modern sense is ABSENT.

Assent — the inner act of agreeing, consenting, or affirming — is ABSENT as a direct biblical term. The concept is present in faith, agreement, and confession vocabulary but never as a standalone psychological category.

Character as a psychological construct — the sum of stable moral and psychological traits — is ABSENT as a single biblical term. Scripture addresses character through the formed inner person, the fruit of the Spirit, and terms for moral quality.

Commitment as a psychological state — the deliberate binding of the will to a person, cause, or covenant — is ABSENT as a single biblical term. The concept is expressed through covenant, vow, and dedication vocabulary.

Conformity — the shaping of the inner person to an external pattern — appears in Scripture in two distinct directions: conformity to Christ (positive) and conformity to the world (negative). The neutral modern term is ABSENT.

Darkening as an inner spiritual/cognitive state — the progressive dimming of spiritual perception — is ABSENT as a single biblical term. The concept is present in specific passages describing the darkened mind or heart.

Spiritual deadness — the condition of the human spirit being non-functional toward God — is ABSENT as a single biblical term. It is expressed through the language of death applied to the inner person.

Emotion as a category does not exist in Hebrew or Greek thought. Ancient languages do not abstract emotions into a general class — they name specific emotions. The concept is ABSENT as a term but present across hundreds of specific instances.

Experience as a general category — the inner accumulation of perceived and felt events — is ABSENT as a biblical term. Scripture addresses specific experiences through suffering, testing, and the knowledge gained through living, but never abstracts them into a general experience category.

Imagination as a faculty of the mind — the capacity to form mental images and conceive possibilities — is ABSENT as a direct biblical term. Scripture addresses imagination primarily in the context of corrupt mental fabrications rather than as a neutral creative faculty.

Intuition as a psychological concept — direct inner knowing without reasoning — is ABSENT as a biblical term. However the concept appears in Scripture under the functions of the human spirit and conscience.

Manipulation as a psychological term — using others for selfish ends through indirect or deceptive means — is ABSENT as a single biblical term. The concept is present across several terms covering its component parts.

Personality as a psychological construct does not exist in biblical vocabulary. The concept is ABSENT as a term. Scripture addresses the formed character of the inner person through related but distinct terms.

Recognition — the inner act of perceiving and identifying something or someone correctly — is ABSENT as a single biblical term. The concept appears in knowledge, acknowledgment, and discernment vocabulary.

Rejection as a psychological inner state — the felt experience of being unwanted or cast away — is ABSENT as a single biblical term. Scripture addresses rejection primarily from the perspective of being rejected by God or by people, with some texts addressing the inner experience.

Sensitivity as a psychological construct — heightened responsiveness to emotional or spiritual stimuli — is ABSENT as a biblical term. The concept appears in Scripture through tender-heartedness, spiritual perception, and the condition of the conscience.

Sexuality as a broad category covering sexual identity, orientation, and expression is a modern construct ABSENT as a single biblical term. Scripture addresses specific sexual acts, desires, and relational covenants rather than sexuality as a category.

Spiritual stupor — a supernaturally induced or sin-produced dullness of spiritual perception — is ABSENT as a modern psychological term in Scripture. The concept appears in specific passages describing a spirit or sleep of deep insensibility.
Surrender as a spiritual concept — the yielding of the will to God — is ABSENT as a single biblical term. The concept is expressed through several yielding and presenting terms.

Transformation as a general category is a modern abstraction. Scripture addresses specific transformation processes through distinct terms. The concept is ABSENT as a single term but richly present across multiple specific terms.

Vulnerability as a psychological concept — the state of being open to being hurt or the willing openness of authentic relationship — is ABSENT as a biblical term. The concept appears in Scripture through weakness, nakedness, and the openness before God.

Transformation as a general category is a modern abstraction. Scripture addresses specific transformation processes through distinct terms. The concept is ABSENT as a single term but richly present across multiple specific terms.

Vulnerability as a psychological concept — the state of being open to being hurt or the willing openness of authentic relationship — is ABSENT as a biblical term. The concept appears in Scripture through weakness, nakedness, and the openness before God.

Spiritual deadness — the condition of the human spirit being non-functional toward God — is ABSENT as a single biblical term. It is expressed through the language of death applied to the inner person.
